title: Lotus alamosanus
taxonomy: Lotus alamosanus is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Lotus, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as trefoils. The classification of Lotus alamosanus is as follows: it is a part of the subgenus Platycarpos, which is characterized by its fruiting pods. It is also a member of the tribe Loteae, a group of plants that are native to the Americas and the Mediterranean region. This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other plants in its family.

intro: Lotus alamosanus, also known as Alamos lotus, is a perennial aquatic plant species belonging to the Nelumbo genus. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Lotus alamosanus include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lotus alamosanus include teas, capsules, and extracts, which are often used to support digestive health and immune function.
